reviews.review_msg: A strict and harsh marker, and will be disrespectful to students. Beware
was a good lecturer and knew how to teach the material well

Irina is the best prof I have ever had at SFU. She runs her class like a navy ship and is intimidating in a good way. I learned a lot in this course and feel like she was very clear. A bit blunt at times, but she is good at what she does.

Dr. K is amazing. Not only was she a great lecturer for a dense course but her exams are fair and she tells you exactly what is expected of you, she also graded and input our grades onto gosfu within a 3 day window. The class is huge and I know she also has another class also. It doesn't get more on the ball than that!

Irina is one of the best profs out there. She does care for teaching, as she wants us to constantly keep up with the course load. She gave us in person lecture prompts and then uploaded it to canvas, tutorial quiz answers were uploaded, and a bunch of practice that came in handy for the final. Final was super easy and overall I am going to miss her

Dr. Kovalyova is amazing, she is caring and wants you to do well, she also provides you with everything you need to succeed. She can come off as tough but that sass also makes her hilarious, her lectures are interesting and she has all sorts of analogies to help you remember, she also tends to emphasize certain things many times. Highly recommend!

Dr. Kovalyova is an amazing and caring professor. Although MBB 231 is a content-heavy course, she made a real effort to simplify the material and explained topics clearly. The tests were fair and reflected what was taught in class. I truly enjoyed taking this course with her

Dr. Kovalyova is an amazing prof. She has amazing analogies that help you understand the content and her slides are very simple but informative enough to understand. She's a little sassy and sarcastic, that could be misinterpreted as being rude, but honestly i think she's hilarious. Lots of hw but it definitely helps you prepare for the final

Dr. K can be strict but it helps you learn. She is compassionate and approachable if you go talk to her in office hours. Exams were fair, nothing unexpected. You have to go to class as there are participation marks.

Decent prof. She does seem to hate the students unintentionally, doesn't really give much leeway for being late to class even if you have a previous class, and kicking someone out on the 2nd lecture didn't feel good. Her test's difficulty isnt super hard. Run-of-the-mill, go to class, tutorials, and office hours.

Sophie is a great professor, she cares about her students and loves to share funny details about her life with us. She is accessible via email outside of class hours, and her exams are generally fair. While she can be slightly disorganized, the class is straightforward and interesting overall.

His 1st time teaching this course, he originally teaches Bisc333. His exams are somewhat fair, though written parts are rather general and less detailed, 2nd mt avg was 10% lower than the first one. Lectures are good with decent notes in powerpoints. And a funny guy. Textbook will help u understand the material better, not to mention problem sets.
I LOVE KINDER MORGAN.

One of the easiest professors I've encountered. Her lectures help clear any questions you may have but heavily based on textbook. Read the text and study by it, and you'll ace the exams. As simple as that.

Lynne is a fabulous prof who has a deep interest in her subject of teaching. ASK QUESTIONS when you are confused and you'll get them answered efficiently and without attitude. She is nice to students and cares how we do. The textbook was essential - EVERY ANSWER was in the textbook. So read ahead and ask questions and you'll do JUUUUST fine.

Great prof. Extremely nice and very clear. Sure her notes are mostly diagrams but that's why you're supposed to read the textbook. Material was interesting. Way better course than MBB 222. Only qualm with Quarmby is that my final mark was 85.5/100 and I ended up with only a B+. How does that happen?
The BEST Prof I have had!!! Very helpful and very nice with her students

The number of "uhh" and "umms" made going to lecture borderline impossible. I ended up counting the number of umms in a 50 minute class and he averaged one every 5-6 seconds. Exams started easy but got progressively more impossible as students told him they were too easy.

I think that Mark cared about the students and he genuinely wanted us to succeed. But, he says the word "uhhh" or "umm" every 6-7 seconds on average (I spent a whole class distracted and ended up counting over 300 ums for 50 min class). Further, is he progressively made the tests harder because some students complained they were too easy.

Im in 201 not 231, I just started this class but I already don't like this guy. His canvas page has barely any resources and for some reason he chooses to not include information in slides and teach off the top of his head. The amount of um's and how confused he makes himself makes it impossible to absorb any information.
Overall, I enjoyed this class, as the prof was approachable when I needed help. The exams were also quite fair and directly from the lectures.
I would say his class was kind of disorganized, as he did not seem to explain the concepts in the slides super well and the exams tested super minor details.

MBB 231 is a dry, material heavy course that requires students to do a lot of rote memorization on their own time. Mike was clear on what would be tested and gave fair exams. Great prof, boring course.

He talks really fast in the lecture which makes it really difficult to understand. There's a lot to memorize. You will need to memorize even the most ridiculous name like glycosylphosphatidylinositol (no abbreviation). The grading scale is also crazily high. You will need 60% to pass and that's just a D.

He is awful. His lecture slides make no sense, he talks way too fast and is awful at explaining concepts. In terms of applying to exams the tutorials were a waste of time. MBB 231 has a lot of material and this course with any prof is time consuming,  but I feel like I had to spend so much more time  than needed on this course because of him

Goes through lecture material VERY fast but his exams are in super simple format, takes questions right out of the textbook. Course material is super dry, LOTS to memorize and read. Overall a very nice guy, willing to help and tutorials were a waste of time. All marks come from exams, no assignments or tutorial grades so be prepared to read

Comments here are really dramatic. In reality Silverman is actually a really good prof and easy to talk to. He gives easy exams because he wants his students to actually feel like theyve learned something. The grading scheme makes it so that the avg is B- just like every other bisc/mbb class. He doesnt deserve this crazy low rating imo.
He is an overall great person. Sure his scale is a little tough, but it is very realistic. The course is easy so for you to stand out it is important to outdo the class.

His lectures aren't very helpful, but he will give you a ton of extra information on the topic that you won't be tested on in class. Grading scheme is bad. Honestly, just do yourself a favor, and take it with a different professor because it will be a lot easier.

Alright, many people find this prof hard because of his grading scheme. Yes, an 87 can sometimes be a B+ in his class but its only cause his class average is around 80%. Its not that hard of a course, its memorization mainly (but if you're in bio what did you expect).
ridiculous grading scheme.

The WORST PROF of SFU! I got 69% overall after all my finals and midterms and he gave me a C-. He has his own grading scheme with +/- S.D. which is completely non sense. AVOID HIM! Shouting at students is his nature and he loves doing that. If you are getting a call from your work or anything urgent dont pick it up becoz he will SLAM you...........
Just terrible professor who does not care about student's future. AVOID

This class was ridiculous. His grading scale is absolutely absurd and makes students who actually do well receive low final grades because he decides to SCALE UP a class that doesn't need to be scaled! Very unapproachable in lecture and outside lecture. He yells at students if he sees their phones instead of actually TEACHING. AVOID HIM.
RUINED MY FUTURE! JUST AWFUL GUY THAT YOU SHOULD AVOID AT ANY COST.. WILL BE MY LOWEST MARK IN MY UNI LIFE CAUSE OF HIS BS GRADING SCHEME .. 70% is a C-
AVOID!! only if youre straight A student and able to get 96%.. since A range start from 96
She is friendly and tells you exactly what's on and not on the exam although monotone . No tricks! Her final is chill as there are only mcqs. She is the best MBB prof I have ever met.

I mean whatever but wiley isn't mandatory when nothing is graded on it. Also, if you write incorrect answers you get marks deducted. The grading scale is not great since 60 is a pass. Also not all exams are weighed the same. But at least the final isn't an actual final.

Amazing prof, she always tells us what you do or do not need to know on the slides. She does fly through most of the slides too but in the end the tests were not hard if you study based on the learning objectives on the slide decks and what she says in class. She was also really funny and laid back!

Very monotone, speaks slow, but tells you EXACTLY what's on the exam. An entertaining and sassy (in a good way) woman who does show up late sometimes. If you need to take further MBB courses after 231, maybe look into another prof. She skips some information that, "we don't need to know", but other people need to know for future MBB courses

AMAZING, anyone who says otherwise just didnt put in work. Tells u exactly whats on exams, repeats things several times to get it to stick in ur head. Slow speaker but otherwise fine. Not sure why theres reviews on not getting through the slide deck... we covered 2 extra chapters and still had classes left over. Just need to know all material well

ingrid is amazing she makes it really clear what you're going to be tested on. i also like that she repeated things often cause it made them stick in my mind. be aware this class is pretty content heavy. i have no complaints other than how ingrid was pretty late to the lecture a lot of the time.

She tells you clearly what you should know for the exams and the exams themselves are purely based on learning objectives. She is very straightforward and her class is easy if you put in the effort. I highly suggest using Wiley Plus.

She's very sweet but is completely inept at using her slides. Half of the classes, she would show up late and then we would only cover a bit of the slide deck we were supposed to. She tells you exactly what's on the exam which is definitely helpful, but if 231 was a difficult course, then she would be a nightmare.

She let any student who had Eras Tour tickets write the 231 final 5 days later to ensure they had a "once in a lifetime opportunity." Her exams are pretty hard but so fair. She always tells you exactly what to study. Most questions come from practice problems/tests. She's an okay lecturer but she's so cool and funny that it kinda makes up for it.
Dr. Northwood is a straightforward prof - lectures are clear, and she gives you the exact info needed to do well on exams. Unstuck study helped with practice quizzes.

She is great. I did not like MBB classes before this one, but she made it interesting. lectures are recorded and she is entertaining to listen to. She is very understanding and kind. definitely take MBB courses with her over many of the other profs.

During lecture she tells you which slides you don't need to focus on (you end up scrapping like 5 slides out of a set which is helpful when it comes to studying). If you study lecture notes=golden for exams. She's very chill and she's even letting students write their finals on different days because it overlaps with the Taylor Swift concert

Love her. Single-handedly restored my faith in MBB. 90% first midterm and 100% on the second.  All I did was take her advice. Keep up with the lecture material, do the assigned textbook questions, and practice problem sets. No tricks on exams..exactly what she said is on it.
very chill prof, for review lectures, she tells you exactly whats going to be on exams, straight forward lectures, and no mandatory attendance

Dr. Northwood is one of, if not the best professor at SFU. Lectures are always engaging and easy to follow, and if you are confused about something, she is more than willing to help you understand a certain topic. Very clear grading criteria, with an easy 100% for 10% of your grade from the Canvas quizzes. Tests are simple and straight forward!

She has a great sense of humour and is extremely clear on what is to be expected on the tests. Can't stress enough how important it is to go over her assigned Wiley+ & Canvas Quiz assignments as they were extremely helpful in preparing for and doing well on exams. She dedicated 1-2 classes for review which was extremely helpful as well!

Horrible Proff. Went to ask a few questions during office hours, he didn't even answer my question. Test are hard and not fair/makes questions on material we never studied and I am a B+ A student in all my other courses. PLS if you don't want your GPA to go down, listen to the advice and don't take the course. On average everyone is at C- in his ca

Dr Hawkins was a really great professor. She put time into helping students and really wanted everyone to succeed. 231 is a content heavy course in itself but Dr Hawkins made the questions really straight forward and provided a good handful of practice. Keep up with material and work hard and you'll do good

Wow if I could give a 0 i would give a 0. Some teachers are truly not meant to be professors and it truly shows through her. Yeah she put an unnecessary amount of stress, worry and depression among the students. Midterm avgs were lower than they've ever been and final was much more brutal. Would be shocked if she doesn't scale with THAT avg.

Exams were relatively strait forward. Material can get overwhelming, so keep up. She asks a few application questions which can skew your grades. The way she gives marks on exams is confusing, sometimes you get full marks other times you don't and asking doesn't help clarify why you did not receive the marks. Overall, shes a good teacher.
Nice and sweet but tests on lots of small fine detailed information such as one sentences from one lecture instead of the whole concept of the lectures.

Lectures are not recorded and she speaks pretty fast but overall I highly recommend this prof. The exams are extremely fair. Study the lecture notes and you'll be fine. The class is overall a lot of material but Nancy does a great job keeping your interest and being organized.
